nodes.label: HighlightedCode
nodes.summary: This project creates a **fault-tolerant syntax highlighter** for terminal applications. It ensures the UI remains responsive by using **asynchronous loading** to fetch the highlighting engine in the background, while a **defensive fallback strategy** guarantees that text is always displayed—downgrading to *Markdown* or plain text—if the highlighter fails or the language is unsupported.

children.label: Defensive Language Fallback
children.summary:
A robust error-handling strategy that ensures the application never crashes due to highlighting errors. It attempts to highlight using the detected file language; if the language is unsupported or the highlighter throws an error, it gracefully downgrades to "markdown" or plain text.

It operates like a "spare tire." If the specialized tire (specific language highlighter) isn't available or blows out (throws an error), the car (application) switches to the generic spare tire (Markdown/Plain text) so it can keep moving without stopping.

children.label: Async Highlighter Loading
children.summary:
A pattern for handling heavy dependencies using React's `Suspense` and `use` hook. The syntax highlighting engine is loaded asynchronously via `getCliHighlightPromise` to prevent blocking the initial UI render.

It acts like ordering food at a busy restaurant. You sit at the table (the component renders) and see a placeholder menu (the fallback plain text) while the kitchen prepares the complex meal (the syntax highlighter loads in the background). Once ready, the "dish" is swapped in automatically.

children.label: Ink Terminal Rendering
children.summary:
The integration layer that translates React components into ANSI escape codes for terminal output. It uses components like `<Ansi>` and `<Text>` to render colored strings directly to standard output.

Think of it as a translator between a web developer and a typewriter. The developer writes code as if they were building a website (using components and props), and this abstraction translates those instructions into the raw keystrokes (ANSI codes) required to display colors and text on a command-line interface.

children.label: Global Highlight Cache
children.summary:
A performance optimization layer that stores previously highlighted code strings in a global `Map` (`hlCache`). Highlighting syntax is computationally expensive, especially during scrolling in a terminal.

Think of it like a "cheat sheet" or a short-term memory buffer. Instead of calculating the syntax colors for the same code block every time it appears on screen, the system looks up the hash of the code and language to instantly retrieve the pre-colored version. It implements a simple eviction policy to keep memory usage low.

children.label: React Compiler Memoization
children.summary:
The code utilizes fine-grained reactivity structures (`_c`, `$`) generated by the React Compiler. Instead of standard `useMemo` hooks, the component uses an array (`$`) to cache specific values and DOM elements, only updating them when their specific dependencies change.

It is like a highly detailed checklist for a factory worker. Instead of rebuilding the entire product when one part changes, the worker checks the list (`if ($[0] !== code)`) and only replaces the specific bolt or screw that is different, saving massive amounts of effort.
